place: Franklin Lakes borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Franklin Lakes was named after William Franklin, the illegitimate son of Benjamin Franklin, who served as New Jersey's last colonial governor.
The borough hosts the headquarters of Becton Dickinson, a medical‑technology firm founded in 1897.
The Franklin Lakes Historical Society was created to preserve the borough’s rich history while looking to its future.
In 1869, the New Jersey Midland Railroad extended to Oakland, building stations that connected Franklin Lakes to the wider region.
Franklin Lakes boasts several homes on the National Register, including the Ackerman–Boyd House and the Blauvelt House, reflecting its 19th‑century heritage.
